regions.note: Perth outer ring (Kwinana $772k, Wellard $690k, Mandurah $560k, Medina $540k) offers strong sub-$800k options. Vacancy 0.6%, population growth 2.2% (highest in AU). REIWA forecasts 10%+ house growth in 2026. Supply critically short. Sub-$800k segment growing fastest.
regions.keyRisk: Rapid price growth may push some suburbs past budget within months

regions.note: Adelaide north (Salisbury +13.1%, Gawler-Two Wells +12.9%) and outer south suburbs under $800k with strong growth. Vacancy 0.5% — tightest in nation. PropTrack forecasts 6-9%, KPMG 8.2%. Sub-$800k segment extremely competitive.
regions.keyRisk: Stamp duty ~$35.5k on $800k = total costs ~$118k, tight vs $110k cash. May need slightly lower price point.
analystForecasts: Cotality: Adelaide leading clearance rates nationally

regions.note: Geraldton median $473k with 24.47% growth. Vacancy sub-1%. Yields 5.5-6%. 10-year average annual growth 12.29%. Very affordable entry. Regional mining/agriculture economy.
regions.keyRisk: Single-industry regional town risk. Small population limits liquidity. Distance from Perth (420km).

regions.note: Albany was highest growth regional area in 2025 (+23.3%). Vacancy below 1%. More diversified economy than Geraldton (agriculture, tourism, port, healthcare). Lifestyle appeal growing.
regions.keyRisk: Regional market — smaller buyer pool. Growth may moderate after 23% spike.
analystForecasts: Cotality: Albany top WA regional performer

regions.note: Townsville median $606k, grew 28.66% in 12mo to Jun 2025. Defence spending (Lavarack Barracks), port expansion, CopperString 2032 energy project. Double-digit growth forecast 2026.
regions.keyRisk: ⚠ SECOND QLD PROPERTY = higher land tax. Cyclone risk. Distance from capital.

regions.note: Mackay median $599k, +24.75% growth. Mining-dependent (Bowen Basin coal). Strong yields. Forecast 3-5% growth in 2026 (moderating from spike).
regions.keyRisk: ⚠ SECOND QLD PROPERTY = higher land tax. Mining cycle risk. Coal dependency.

regions.note: Darwin median $710k, vacancy 0.6%. Defence spending, Ship Lift Facility, mining. Forecast +10.5% houses 2026. Recovering from decade-long downturn. Diversification from QLD.
regions.keyRisk: Cyclone risk. Small market = low liquidity. FIFO/defence dependent. Strata insurance costs high.

regions.note: Bunbury median ~$550k, vacancy 1.33%. Forecast 10% growth 2026. Good lifestyle regional, 2hrs from Perth. Infrastructure investment growing.
regions.keyRisk: Regional market. Vacancy slightly higher than Perth (1.33% vs 0.6%).

regions.note: Hobart median $710k, vacancy 0.5% (extremely tight). Modest +5.4% growth forecast. Rebuilding gradually. Tourism + lifestyle appeal. Affordable vs mainland capitals.
regions.keyRisk: Slow growth momentum. Small economy. Limited employment diversity.

regions.note: Logan/Ipswich median pushing $780-850k. Vacancy <1%. Strong yields 4.5-5.2%. Olympic 2032 infrastructure. But Brisbane median now $1.175M pulling everything up.
regions.keyRisk: ⚠ SECOND QLD PROPERTY = higher land tax. Median approaching/exceeding budget. Crime perception in some Logan suburbs.

regions.note: Melbourne outer suburbs $500-700k with Melton at $510k. Growth 5-7% forecast but Melbourne lagging other capitals. Vacancy higher (1.4% city-wide). State's fastest-growing population corridors.
regions.keyRisk: Melbourne underperforming nationally. High stamp duty ($43k on $800k = over budget on costs). Vacancy not as tight.

regions.note: Geelong benefits from $5.5B infrastructure (convention centre, Avalon Airport, Fast Rail). Median approaching $800k. Strong fundamentals but VIC stamp duty kills it for investors.
regions.keyRisk: VIC stamp duty ~$43k on $800k = total costs $123k, exceeds $110k cash. Growth below WA/SA.
analystForecasts: Cotality: Greater Geelong top 3 VIC investment

regions.note: Mount Gambier $530k, +11.34% growth. Affordable but small regional town. Limited economic diversity. Yield 3.66% (low for a regional).
regions.keyRisk: Small population, low liquidity. Limited growth catalysts beyond affordability.

regions.note: Ballarat $550k, accessible from Melbourne. Diversified economy (education, healthcare, gov). Historic appeal. But VIC stamp duty is punitive for investors and growth is modest.
regions.keyRisk: VIC stamp duty ~$35k on $550k + surcharge for investors. Melbourne commuter dependency.
analystForecasts: Cotality: Ballarat leads regional VIC revival

regions.priceFilter: BORDERLINE — median $500-750k varies by suburb but trending up
regions.note: Newcastle median approaching $1M for houses. Some suburbs still $500-750k. Strong fundamentals (university, hospital, port). But NSW stamp duty high and median moving past budget.
regions.keyRisk: NSW stamp duty ~$30k on $800k. Median rising fast, shrinking sub-$800k options.

regions.note: Wollongong median $1.09M — well over $800k budget. Market 'soft' conditions. Not viable at current budget.
regions.keyRisk: Price too high. Soft conditions.

regions.note: Sydney median $1.75M — more than double budget. Not viable.
regions.keyRisk: Completely unaffordable at $800k budget.

regions.note: Canberra median $1.05M — over budget. Vacancy 1.0% (looser than other capitals). Growth modest.
regions.keyRisk: Over budget. Government employment concentration risk.

regions.note: Brisbane median $1.175M — well over budget. Plus second QLD property = higher land tax.
regions.keyRisk: Over budget AND land tax penalty.

regions.note: Extremely affordable ($270-280k) with yields 7%+. But single-industry towns with limited growth prospects. Low liquidity, hard to sell.
regions.keyRisk: Single industry (steel/mining). Population decline risk. Very low liquidity.

summary: For a $800k budget growth investor diversifying away from QLD: Perth mid/outer ring (score 9.2) and Adelaide north/south (score 8.8) are the clear top picks. Both have sub-$800k suburbs, extremely tight vacancy (0.5-0.6%), strong growth forecasts (8-10%+), and provide state diversification. WA regional (Geraldton, Albany) offers higher growth but with regional market risks. Darwin is an interesting diversification play at $710k median. Avoid Sydney, Canberra, Brisbane inner — all well over budget. Melbourne is affordable but underperforming with punitive stamp duty.
avoids: QLD — Brisbane inner — over budget + land tax penalty
NSW — Wollongong — over budget + soft market
